main_event: Multiple civilians in Moorhead, Minnesota and Fargo, North Dakota observed 5 glowing reddish-orange disc-shaped objects in V-formation, 5 flights at 8-minute intervals, traveling south to north, each lasting ~4 seconds, between 2100-2145 hours on 25 April 1952; investigating officer personally staked out next night from 10th floor of Black Building and positively identified objects as migrating Mallard and Teal Ducks reflecting neon city lights.
sighted_object: round/disc; reddish-orange (bright red and orange); 'a glowing ball'; 5 per flight in wide V-formation; sloppy V — objects appeared to have trouble maintaining constant speed and altitude; no trail, exhaust, or sound; moving very high and very fast
length_of_observation: 4 seconds per flight; total event 2100-2145 (5 flights at 8-min intervals)
witness_description: Patrolman [REDACTED], Moorhead Police Dept.; Mrs. [REDACTED] (3 women from Moorhead Minn); Al [REDACTED], Fargo Fire Department; [REDACTED], KFGO radio station Fargo ND; Doctor [REDACTED], ND Agricultural College Fargo ND — 7 named adult witnesses; Appendix I states 8 total observers
interesting_points: Exemplary self-contained investigation: reporting officer (Capt. Gadeois, Det. 2, 462nd GOS) personally staked out Black Building 10th floor the following night (28 Apr) with binoculars, high-speed camera, and telescope
Positive identification of Teal Ducks via flapping wings (visual) at 2115 hours on 28 April; Geese also identified by honking
Explanation: phosphorescence from neon city lights picked up by fowl feathers from ground level with lighting glare — elevated vantage point revealed true nature
Initial report (27 Apr) explicitly filed as hearsay from citizens; follow-up report (29 Apr) filed as first-hand observed data
V-formation 'sloppiness' with varying speed and altitude is consistent with duck flocking behavior
Northwest Airlines commercial flight noted at ~2110 hours on approach — seen by majority of same observers; correctly identified as conventional aircraft at the time
Record card NUMBER OF OBJECTS: 'varied' — form uses varying to indicate 5 discs per flight across 5 flights = 25 total objects, or simply 5 per flight
